run_summary: Screen 1 faces a portfolio decision pass with 7 open positions (all at or near the 15-day holding window) and a new basket of 10 flags from two AI-catalyst triggers. Four open positions have hit day 15 and must EXIT per discipline; the remainder are near window close. New flags split across two triggers: OpenAI voice models (6 flags) and Claude Cowork expansion (4 flags). The Cowork basket contains three names (ONTO, AEIS, PLAB) with "none" threat_assessment and crisp filings evidence of positive mechanical bearing — these qualify for conviction tier, but the basket rule applies: default all three to exploratory, with ONTO potentially elevated to conviction as the marginal standout.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
entries.thesis: A coding-agent product from Anthropic has zero mechanical bearing on demand for advanced-node process control equipment. If anything, more AI chips (which is what Cowork-style demand ultimately drives) means more wafer starts at leading nodes, which means MORE metrology/inspection tools. The five-day drawdown makes this cheaper than a clean sympathy-only setup, but there's no filings-based reason to think the trigger event actually impairs the thesis.
entries.what_kills: A leading-edge fab capex cut announcement, a WFE downgrade cycle from Applied/LAM/KLAC, or an ONTO-specific customer-loss 8-K within the fade window.
entries.reasoning: Claude Cowork trigger basket: 4 flags (ONTO, AEIS, PLAB, CGNX). Three names (ONTO, AEIS, PLAB) have threat_assessment=none + crisp filings evidence that the catalyst is mechanically positive or neutral. Basket rule normally forces exploratory, BUT ONTO stands out: (1) threat_assessment=none (not minimal), (2) filings explicitly frame AI as a demand TAILWIND via increased capex/infrastructure investment, not a threat, (3) five-day change (-20.35%) has made the price substantially cheaper than the sympathy-only setup warrants, (4) confidence=4 and the specific mechanism is strongest among the three. ONTO is the marginal conviction candidate; the other two (AEIS, PLAB) default to exploratory to respect basket rule and avoid 60% bankroll concentration on the Cowork thesis.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case correctly identifies that AI capex tailwinds should support semiconductor equipment demand, but the five-day drawdown of -20.35% suggests either a structural repricing of the semi-cap cycle or customer-specific bad news that the flag has not yet surfaced.
red_team.critique: The -20.35% five-day decline in ONTO is material and inconsistent with a simple sympathy-fade narrative on a Cowork news item. If the logical case for ONTO (more AI chips = more capex = more tools) is sound, the depth of the move suggests either: (1) the market is pricing a semi-cap cycle downturn that goes beyond sympathy, or (2) there is customer concentration risk (leading-edge fab capex cuts) that has not been disclosed yet. The filings evidence of positive framing is noted, but price action warrants caution on conviction tier sizing.

entries.name: Blackbaud, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
entries.thesis: OpenAI's voice model release has no plausible mechanism to displace donor-management workflows, integrated payments, or peer-to-peer fundraising platforms. Blackbaud's own Risk Factors explicitly warn that stock price may decline on AI market perception 'regardless of our actual operating performance' — this is exactly the pattern we're seeing today.
entries.what_kills: A subsequent 8-K disclosing customer churn, a security incident update, or renewal-cohort weakness in the ~40% of contracts up for renewal in 2026.
entries.reasoning: OpenAI voice trigger basket: 6 flags total, triggers basket rule. BLKB: threat_assessment=minimal, panic_calibration=unjustified, filings evidence is clean (AI as perception risk, not operational threat; core moat is subscription + embedded payments + security moat). Confidence=4, but basket rule forces exploratory tier. 6% position sizing.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case assumes the 5-day decline of -3.34% is purely sympathy-driven, but below-average volume (0.58x) may reflect genuine repositioning among institutional holders worried about vertical SaaS exposure to AI disruption, even if the specific threat is low.
red_team.critique: The flag's own Risk Factor language acknowledges that BLKB stock may decline 'regardless of actual operating performance' on AI perception—but this does not prove the current decline IS purely perception-driven. The below-average volume could reflect thin liquidity masking real conviction selling by informed holders. The thesis that voice models pose 'zero product overlap' is sound, but it doesn't address whether institutional SaaS exposure rotation is self-reinforcing over the next 5-10 days regardless of fundamentals.

entries.name: nCino, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
entries.thesis: OpenAI's voice model release does not threaten bank loan origination or onboarding workflows — these require regulatory audit trails, KYC/AML compliance, and integration with core banking systems that a voice agent cannot replicate. Move is thematic AI-SaaS aversion, not substantive.
entries.what_kills: Evidence that a major FI customer is piloting a competing LLM-based loan origination platform, or a downgrade tied to AI displacement risk.
entries.reasoning: OpenAI voice trigger basket (6 flags). NCNO: threat_assessment=minimal, panic_calibration=unjustified. Filings confirm AI risk is about LLM-driven loan origination (structural risk, not voice-specific); actual customers are FIs with 3-5 year contracts on regulated platforms (Salesforce). Voice agents do not displace KYC/AML/audit trails. Confidence=4, basket rule → exploratory.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case is strongly supported by the positive five-day change (+6.06%), which contradicts the 'sympathy fade' framing and suggests any single-day dip was shallow and already reversing.
red_team.critique: The five-day change is +6.06%, meaning NCNO has already recovered from the one-day -3.99% move and is up on a 5-day basis. This directly contradicts the sympathy-fade setup; the market has already repriced and rejected the AI-agent threat to loan origination. The bull thesis survives not because the reasoning is airtight, but because price action has already validated it.

entries.name: Paycom Software, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
entries.thesis: Voice models don't file 941s, run payroll ACH, or handle multi-state UI compliance. Paycom's moat is regulatory infrastructure and being the bank-of-record for employee comp — a text/voice interface doesn't displace that. Five-day change is still strongly positive, so this is a single-day sympathy dip.
entries.what_kills: An unusual client-loss disclosure or evidence that agentic AI is being adopted for payroll compliance in a regulated context.
entries.reasoning: OpenAI voice trigger basket (6 flags). PAYC: threat_assessment=minimal, panic_calibration=unjustified. Filings discuss AI as general disruptive risk but core business is payroll tax/ACH/compliance infrastructure with embedded banking relationships. Voice models do not file 941s. Five-day change (+10.2%) confirms this is a thematic dip, not real repricing. Confidence=4, basket rule → exploratory.

red_team.weakest_link: The five-day change of +10.2% already fully refutes the sympathy-fade setup, making the BUY call reactive rather than anticipatory—by the time the trade is executed, the repricing has already happened.
red_team.critique: PAYC is up 10.2% over five days despite today's -3.67% move, proving the market has already rejected the AI-voice threat to payroll compliance. The current entry point is not a sympathy dip; it is a stock in the middle of a recovery. The bull thesis is not wrong about the no-threat mechanism, but the trade itself is late—the repricing the flag anticipates has already occurred.

entries.name: Paylocity Holding Corporation
entries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
entries.thesis: Same thesis as PAYC — voice models don't handle payroll tax filings, ACH banking relationships, or state-by-state employment law compliance. The five-day change is still strongly positive, confirming this is a one-day thematic dip on the OpenAI headline.
entries.what_kills: A negative preannouncement or evidence that agentic AI is displacing HR SaaS in Paylocity's SMB/mid-market segment.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: OpenAI voice trigger basket (6 flags). PCTY: threat_assessment=minimal, panic_calibration=unjustified. Same thesis as PAYC: payroll tax/compliance core moat is untouched by voice agents. Filings acknowledge AI competitive risk generically but no product-replacement vector identified. Five-day change (+10.22%) strongly positive, confirming sympathy dip only. Confidence=4, basket rule → exploratory.

red_team.weakest_link: Like PAYC, the five-day change of +10.22% shows the repricing has already occurred, making this a reactive entry into a stock already in recovery mode rather than a prospective fade-catch.
red_team.critique: PCTY is up 10.22% over five days despite today's -3.05% move. The sympathy-dip thesis is already disproven by price action. The BUY is not timing a fade; it is entering a stock that has already recovered most of the one-day sympathy damage. The fundamental thesis (payroll tax compliance moat is untouched) is sound, but the tactical entry is late.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
entries.catalyst: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
entries.thesis: AEIS's Risk Factors flag AI as a demand TAILWIND, not a threat. A Claude coding-agent product doesn't compete with power conversion for fabs or data centers — the opposite: agentic workloads driving inference demand require more of exactly what AEIS sells. The five-day drawdown has made this cheaper still.
entries.what_kills: A specific AEIS customer (Applied Materials, LAM) issues a capex cut, a large data-center hyperscaler pauses buildout, or an AEIS 8-K reveals an operational issue.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Claude Cowork trigger basket (4 flags). AEIS: threat_assessment=none, panic_calibration=unjustified, filings explicitly cite AI as a demand driver for power delivery to data centers and fabs. This is strong evidence (crisp filings language). Confidence=4 and five-day pullback (-17.35%) makes it cheap. However, basket rule applies: ONTO already elevated to conviction (marginal standout on filings + price depth), so AEIS defaults to exploratory (6%) to keep total Cowork basket exposure disciplined.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case relies on filings evidence that AI is a demand tailwind for power delivery, but the -17.35% five-day move suggests the market may be pricing structural competitive or capex-cycle headwinds that the flag has not engaged.
red_team.critique: A -17.35% five-day decline on a Claude Cowork news event (which should be mechanically positive for power infrastructure) is disproportionate to a simple sympathy fade. This either reflects a broader data-center or semi-capex cycle deterioration not yet disclosed, or genuine competitive pressure from alternative power solutions. The filings language is reassuring on AI as a tailwind, but the magnitude of the move suggests either the market is skeptical or has information the flag has not incorporated.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
entries.thesis: Every chip that exists — including every accelerator that runs Claude Cowork inference — requires photomasks. There's no mechanism by which an application-layer AI agent reduces mask demand; it increases it. Panic on this news is mechanically backwards. Curation hypothesis confirmed by filings.
entries.what_kills: A specific customer-concentration event (top-2 customers are 29% of revenue) — if one of them cuts orders. Or an escalation of China export controls hitting Photronics's two China fabs directly.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Claude Cowork trigger basket (4 flags). PLAB: threat_assessment=none, panic_calibration=unjustified, filings show zero AI competitive risk (photomask supply is a hardware input layer, not threatened by application-layer agents). Confidence=4. Thesis is crisp: every chip requires masks, more agentic inference = more chips = more masks. Five-day change (-11.92%) is material but less extended than AEIS/ONTO. Basket rule forces exploratory (6%) to avoid conviction over-concentration on Cowork thesis.

red_team.weakest_link: The photomask-demand thesis is sound (every chip needs masks, more AI silicon = more masks), but the -11.92% five-day decline, combined with extreme customer concentration (top 2 = 29% of revenue), suggests either a top-customer loss is in play or the market is pricing China export controls or capex cuts harder than the flag acknowledges.
red_team.critique: PLAB's 29% customer concentration (top 2) is material risk that the flag mentions only in the 'what_kills' section. The -11.92% five-day move is not explained by sympathy alone; it likely reflects either a customer-specific event or broader China-related supply-chain repricing. The filings evidence on mask demand being design-driven is correct, but customer concentration on photomask supply is a known structural vulnerability in this cycle.

entries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
entries.thesis: OpenAI's real-time voice conversation capability is more directly relevant to Duolingo than to most names in this basket — but Duolingo already offers AI-powered voice conversation practice (Duolingo Max), and the core moat is the streak/gamification dopamine loop, not instructional depth. The five-day change is still strongly positive, and today's move is a modest thematic dip, not a real repricing.
entries.what_kills: MAU/DAU deceleration data or evidence that Duolingo Max subscriber growth is stalling as users shift to native ChatGPT voice for language practice — this would elevate to 'partial' or 'justified'.
entries.reasoning: OpenAI voice trigger basket. DUOL: threat_assessment=indirect (not minimal, not none), confidence=3 (at minimum threshold). Filings DO acknowledge GenAI as disruptive risk ('new product could gain rapid scale') and improved translation as a threat. DUOL Max (AI-powered conversation tier) is directly exposed. This is the one name in the OpenAI voice basket where the threat is non-trivial. Exploratory tier (6%) is justified, but the indirect threat assessment makes confidence marginal. WATCH allows re-evaluation after more data.

entries.name: Health Catalyst, Inc
entries.catalyst: OpenAI releases new voice models for more natural live conversations
entries.thesis: Voice AI has no plausible application to healthcare provider analytics/data warehousing. However, HCAT has real company-specific issues (Project Nexus restructuring, CEO transition, DOS-to-Ignite migration churn) that make it hard to isolate the sympathy-fade signal from operational headwinds. Modest confidence and small position size warranted.
entries.what_kills: Any incremental negative on Project Nexus execution, DOS migration churn, or a downgrade — HCAT has real issues that could dominate the tape independent of AI sentiment.
entries.reasoning: OpenAI voice trigger basket. HCAT: confidence=2 (below min_buy_confidence=3). While threat_assessment=minimal and panic_calibration=unjustified, HCAT has real company-specific headwinds (Project Nexus restructuring, CEO transition, DOS migration churn) that make it hard to isolate sympathy fade from operational deterioration. At confidence 2, risk/reward is skewed to noise. Skip.

entries.name: Cognex Corporation
entries.catalyst: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
entries.thesis: Cognex has real, filings-acknowledged AI competition — but it's from open-source machine-vision models on factory floors, NOT from a coding agent for office productivity. Claude Cowork does not do deterministic sub-millisecond machine vision on a QA line. The panic is thematic, applying a general 'AI eats software' narrative to a very specific factory-floor workflow. The filings acknowledge the general threat but the trigger event itself does nothing to accelerate it.
entries.what_kills: A major factory-automation customer (auto OEM, EV battery, life sciences) publicly switching to a general-purpose AI vision solution; or an open-source model demonstrating deterministic latency parity in an industrial-grade setting.
entries.reasoning: Claude Cowork trigger basket (4 flags). CGNX: threat_assessment=indirect (not none, not minimal), confidence=3 (at threshold). Filings DO acknowledge AI as a competitive risk to machine-vision market entry ('large tech companies may distribute free or low-cost solutions'); CGNX frames it as both threat AND opportunity, but the threat is real. Claude Cowork does not directly target factory-floor QA, but the filings confirm that open-source ML vision is a live competitive pressure. This is the weakest entry in the Cowork basket. WATCH pending more evidence that the trigger actually accelerates the identified filings risk.
